name: Morrighan Bustlewing
summary: She arrived in Karnuk carrying the cold breath of the Unseelie Court and left behind a city that had learned death was not the worst thing that could happen to it.
content:
## Appearance

Morrighan is Small in the technical sense and enormous in every other one. She has the wings of something that died and decided to keep flying anyway — pale iridescent blue at odds with the deep purple of her skin and the absolute darkness of her hair, which falls in loose waves around pointed ears and a face that is, somehow, placidly delighted. She wears armor that seems too serious for something her size. She carries herself like she's already at peace with how this ends.

## Personality

She is the calmest person in most rooms, and the most unsettling. Morrighan does not perform darkness — she is simply not afraid of what frightens everyone else, and her equanimity in the face of horror reads, to those who don't know her, as either great wisdom or great damage. Her morbid humor arrives with the precision of someone who has thought about death so long that it's become genuinely funny. She is not cruel. She is merely unhurried. She knows where everyone ends up.

## Backstory

She was raised in the Shadowglade among fairies who worshipped Morrith, the death goddess — who saw passing not as tragedy but as transition. When Lady Malfaria, Queen of the Unseelie Court, appeared in her dreams and offered her a role, Morrighan said yes with both eyes open. She is the Rave'n's Herald. She spreads the Unseelie Court's cold influence through the mortal world not out of malice but because she genuinely believes the chill of death is a gift the living keep refusing.

## Role in the Party

Morrighan is the party's unsettling center of gravity — the one whose agenda is most opaque, whose affect is hardest to read, who has aligned with this group for reasons that may or may not survive the campaign's turning points. She provides power that others can't replicate and a particular brand of social navigation: she can be charming when she chooses to be, terrifying when she doesn't. The tension in her relationships comes from her treating warmth as a tactic rather than a truth.

## Chronicle

*The historians who came after could not agree whether Morrighan Bustlewing was a villain, a herald, or something the existing categories simply failed to contain. She arrived in Karnuk carrying the cold breath of the Unseelie Court and left behind a city that had learned, however briefly, that death was not the worst thing that could happen to it.*
patron: Lady Malfaria, Queen of the Unseelie Court
features: Form of Dread (3/LR — temp HP, frighten on hit, immune to frightened)
Pact of the Chain — Find Familiar

personality.traits: Embraces the macabre with genuine serenity. Her dark humor is not performance — it is philosophy.
personality.ideals: Death is the truest part of the cycle. She serves it not from nihilism but from a kind of reverence.
personality.bonds: She is the Rave'n's Herald. Lady Malfaria chose her for this. That is not an honor — it is an identity.
personality.flaws: She views relationships as instruments, people as tools. This is her most dangerous blind spot.

moments.title: The Grove Entrance
moments.description: "I am here to guide you through these forsaken depths, to ensure that death claims only what it must… or perhaps, what it should not." First words. Meant every one.

moments.title: The Inscription
moments.description: Read the ancient Feywild inscription aloud, earning the party access to the sanctuary once every seven days. The Queen of the Seelie Court appeared. Morrighan did not seem surprised.

moments.title: Moonlight on Water
moments.description: In the ambush, she moved through the chaos like moonlight on water — spells slicing through flesh and doubt alike while mercenaries fell around her.
